title: FIFA World Cup Group A Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group A in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico's co-host status and daunting Estadio Azteca opener against South Africa on June 11 have solidified trader consensus at 46.5% implied probability to win Group A, bolstered by Javier Aguirre's tactical clarity in recent closed-door camps fusing Liga MX intensity with European stars like Edson Álvarez and a rising generation of attacking midfielders. Czechia (23.5%) and South Korea (21.5%) trail closely as competitive challengers, with the Czechs' UEFA playoff grit—highlighted by Tomáš Souček's leadership—and South Korea's consistent qualifying form under Son Heung-min fueling upset potential amid altitude prep. South Africa's resilient Bafana Bafana defense caps them at 5.2%, facing steep odds in a balanced group defined by recent training sharpness and no major injury disruptions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Switzerland's trader consensus lead at 52.5% to win FIFA World Cup 2026 Group B stems from their consistent tournament pedigree—reaching the round of 16 in four of the last five World Cups—bolstered by Granit Xhaka's midfield control and elite defensive organization shown in recent friendlies like the narrow 4-3 loss to Germany in late March. Canada's 25% implied probability reflects host-nation momentum under Jesse Marsch, with Alphonso Davies in peak form during Toronto base camp sessions and home-soil openers against Bosnia and Herzegovina on June 12. Bosnia's 21.5% surge follows their dramatic UEFA playoff victory over Italy on March 31, fueling physical set-piece threats and never-say-die spirit in high-altitude Salt Lake City training. Qatar trails at 2.5%, hampered by comparatively weaker Asian qualifying form despite solid technical discipline in Santa Barbara prep.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Brazil as a 71.5% implied probability to top FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C, driven by their five-time champion history, elite attacking depth featuring Vinícius Jr. and Endrick, and dominant CONMEBOL qualifiers marked by top possession and goal metrics. Morocco's 17.5% reflects their 2022 semifinal breakthrough, rock-solid defense with fewest goals conceded in African qualifiers, and lethal transitions led by Hakimi and Díaz. Scotland (5.3%) gains traction from physical midfielders like McTominay and set-piece strength after playoff qualification and a 28-year return, while Haiti (1.8%) remains a longshot despite pacey forwards. Recent base camp updates confirm positive fitness across squads, with Brazil and Scotland thriving in U.S. facilities ahead of the June 13 Brazil-Morocco opener at MetLife Stadium.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D pricing reflects a fiercely competitive group stage race, with Türkiye's 37.5% implied probability slightly ahead of USA's 32% due to their gritty March playoff triumph over Kosovo and explosive young talents like Arda Güler and Kenan Yıldız complementing Hakan Çalhanoğlu's playmaking. USA's home-soil advantage across venues like SoFi Stadium bolsters their case, tempered by a 2-1 friendly loss to Türkiye last June despite narrow 2-1 wins over Australia and Paraguay. Paraguay's CONMEBOL qualifier defensive stinginess and Miguel Almirón's counters, paired with Australia's Harry Souttar-led physicality and set-piece threat, position them as spoilers in a matchup defined by tight head-to-head history and contrasting styles—technical flair versus grit—keeping advancement paths wide open for the top two or best third.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters as the clear trader consensus favorite at 72.5% implied probability to win Group E, bolstered by their elite FIFA ranking, dominant UEFA qualifying campaign with top possession and defensive metrics, and deep squad featuring stars like Jamal Musiala and Joshua Kimmich, positioning them to handle the expanded 48-team format's group stage demands where top two plus best thirds advance. Ecuador's 15.5% reflects their CONMEBOL qualifiers' stingy defense—fewest goals conceded—and high-pressing style under coach Sebastián Beccacece, making them viable for second amid recent training camp cohesion at Ohio State. Ivory Coast (9%) leverages athletic midfielders like Franck Kessié and Sébastien Haller for counter threats post-CAF playoffs, while Curaçao's 2.5% underscores debutant status and recent friendly concessions despite gritty CONCACAF path, with no major injuries reported across camps in late April.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Netherlands at 55.5% implied probability to win Group F, driven by their unbeaten UEFA qualifying campaign—capped by a 4-0 rout of Lithuania—and solid friendlies like a 2-1 victory over Norway on March 27, bolstered by stars like Virgil van Dijk and Frenkie de Jong amid high FIFA rankings and three-time finalist pedigree. Tunisia lags at 5.0% as the clear underdog, relying on gritty African qualifying but lacking firepower against Europe's elite.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Belgium's elite FIFA ranking, dominant UEFA qualification campaign topped by a 7-0 rout of Liechtenstein last November, and recent friendly results—a 5-2 thrashing of the USA and 1-1 draw with Mexico in late March—solidify their 70.5% implied probability as Group G winners, reflecting trader consensus on their attacking depth despite an aging golden generation. Egypt trails at 14.5% buoyed by Mohamed Salah's form and convincing CAF Group A triumph, plus March friendlies including a 4-0 win over Saudi Arabia, positioning them as realistic challengers in a favorable draw. Iran's 7.2% stems from AFC playoff success and a 5-0 friendly demolition of Costa Rica, while New Zealand's 3.2% underscores their 85th ranking and Oceania auto-qualifier status as longshots against superior opposition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's elite FIFA ranking (No. 2), Euro 2024 triumph, and dominant friendlies—including a 5-0 win on April 18 and 3-0 over Serbia on March 27—have solidified trader consensus at 79.5% implied probability to win Group H, bolstered by Lamine Yamal's expected recovery from hamstring injury for the June 15 opener versus Cape Verde. Uruguay's gritty CONMEBOL qualifying campaign and recent draws against England (1-1) and Algeria (0-0) position them as competitive challengers at 12.5%, leveraging stars like Federico Valverde amid prep camps in Mexico. Saudi Arabia's counterattacking threat, recalling their 2022 upset, and Cape Verde's historic debut sustain slim 2.4% and 1.1% chances, respectively, with all squads reporting full fitness in ongoing U.S.-based training.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads Group I trader consensus at 68.5% implied probability to win the FIFA World Cup 2026 group stage, driven by unmatched squad depth featuring Kylian Mbappé's elite finishing, dominant UEFA qualifying form, and tactical versatility under Deschamps, positioning them to navigate a "group of death" with Senegal, Norway, and Iraq. Norway follows at 22% thanks to Erling Haaland's clinical counter-attacking threat and Martin Ødegaard's creativity, bolstered by strong recent training camp fitness in Greensboro. Senegal's 9% reflects their physical pressing and Sadio Mané's leadership despite a tough opener versus France on June 16, while Iraq's 2.7% underscores their underdog status post-historic playoff qualification after 40 years, with disciplined counters led by Aymen Hussein. Recent April 23-25 camp updates confirm no major injuries across squads, sustaining current positioning amid high spirits and tactical drills.

eventMetadata.context_description: Argentina's status as defending champions and FIFA's No. 3-ranked side overwhelmingly drives trader consensus at 75.5% implied probability to top Group J, bolstered by Lionel Messi's confirmed participation in what could be his final World Cup and a flawless CONMEBOL qualifying campaign. Austria, at 15%, emerges as the credible challenger after a dominant UEFA playoff run under Ralf Rangnick's high-pressing system, ranking No. 24 with strong recent form against Algeria (No. 28) and debutants Jordan (No. 63). Algeria's preparations kicked off with a May 25 training camp announcement and friendlies versus Netherlands, while Jordan reports no injuries ahead of their historic bow; no major injury updates or withdrawals have shifted sentiment in the past week, keeping lower probabilities intact for the underdogs amid the expanded format where top two plus best thirds advance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Portugal leads Group K trader consensus at 67.5% implied probability as the clear favorite, bolstered by their dominant UEFA qualifying campaign—topping Group F with a 9-1 rout of Armenia—and recent friendlies including a 2-0 win over the USMNT on March 31, showcasing depth beyond 41-year-old Cristiano Ronaldo with Bruno Fernandes anchoring midfield. Colombia sits at 26.5%, reflecting CONMEBOL strength and head-to-head potential, though James Rodríguez's early April dehydration hospitalization (resolved by April 7) introduces minor uncertainty alongside adductor issues for Juan Cabal. DR Congo's 7.3% stems from playoff heroics, a 1-0 extra-time victory over Jamaica on March 31 marking their first World Cup since 1974, while Uzbekistan's 0.7% underscores their debutant status despite solid AFC qualification via penalty shootout wins. With fixtures opening Portugal vs. DR Congo and Uzbekistan vs. Colombia on June 17, recent form drives the hierarchy amid neutral-site group stage dynamics.

Ghana's 6% stems from Carlos Queiroz's fresh appointment and physical style, while debutant Panama lags at 1.8% lacking depth against top competition; top two advance from this 'Group of Death.'

title: Which continent will win the 2026 Fifa World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 72.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's 16 qualification slots filled with pre-tournament favorites like Spain, France, England, Germany, Netherlands, and Portugal, bolstered by their depth from recent European Championship successes and FIFA rankings dominance. South America's 21% reflects CONMEBOL powerhouses Argentina (defending champions) and Brazil securing spots amid a competitive qualifiers campaign ending October 2025. The March 31 finals saw UEFA surprises like Italy's penalty shootout exit to Bosnia and Herzegovina but reinforced continental strength with newcomers Sweden, Turkiye, and Czechia advancing. Lower odds for Africa (2.8%), Asia (2.1%), North America (2.1%), and Oceania (0.3%) align with fewer slots, limited deep tournament runs, and group draw challenges despite expanded 48-team field.

title: World Cup game relocated away from Mexico?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is announced that any 2026 FIFA World Cup match originally scheduled to be held in Mexico will be relocated to a location outside of Mexico by June 10, 2026, 11:59 ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement will immediately resolve the market to "Yes" regardless of whether the relocation is later revoked.

The resolution source will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: World Cup game relocated away from Mexico?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is announced that any 2026 FIFA World Cup match originally scheduled to be held in Mexico will be relocated to a location outside of Mexico by June 10, 2026, 11:59 ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement will immediately resolve the market to "Yes" regardless of whether the relocation is later revoked.

The resolution source will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus at 95.7% "No" reflects FIFA's firm commitment to the original 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule across Mexico's host cities—Estadio Azteca in Mexico City, Estadio Akron in Guadalajara, and Estadio BBVA in Monterrey—despite earlier cartel violence prompting postponements of domestic matches and calls for relocation in February. Recent developments, including the revamped Azteca's reopening last week, Mexico's deployment plans for nearly 100,000 officers, and FIFA's rejection of Iran's venue-switch request, have solidified confidence in proceeding as planned. Venue renovations face tight deadlines but show progress, with no official changes announced. Realistic shifts could arise from severe security escalations invoking FIFA's relocation clause or confirmed stadium unreadiness, though preparations indicate low risk.

title: Which team will replace Iran at FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve in favor of the men’s national team that is confirmed by FIFA to replace the Football Federation Islamic Republic of Iran (IR) at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If an unlisted men’s national team is officially announced to replace Iran at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, this market will resolve to “Other”

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is no confirmation that any team will replace Iran within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “No Replacement”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no replacement for Iran in FIFA World Cup 2026 Group G at 69.5%, reflecting FIFA's firm stance on sporting merit for qualification and recent rejections of political interference, including a Trump envoy's suggestion last week to slot in Italy—which Italian officials dismissed as inappropriate despite the Azzurri's failure to qualify via UEFA playoffs. Iran's Sports Minister cited ongoing Middle East conflict from U.S.-Israel airstrikes in March as barring participation, yet FIFA President Gianni Infantino and U.S. officials affirmed Iranian players' welcome, bolstering the status quo. UAE leads alternatives at 20.1% as AFC's next-best qualifier behind Iran, with Italy lingering at 10.5% on residual speculation, while others like Bolivia remain negligible amid confederation slot precedents.
